subject: Cloud Computing
title: Container Platforms as PaaS
description: A micro-lesson that makes Container Platforms as PaaS usable.

hook.text: You've containerized your app. Now you need somewhere to run it without managing cluster infrastructure.

buildup.text: Services like ECS, Cloud Run, and Azure Container Apps run containers for you. No cluster to manage.

discovery.text: You push a container image and the platform handles scaling, networking, and health checks.

twist.text: These services blur the line between PaaS and serverless. You manage images, not servers or VMs.

climax.text: Container PaaS is the sweet spot: more control than serverless, less work than running Kubernetes.

punchline.text: Ship a container. The platform handles the rest.

quiz.question: How do container PaaS platforms differ from running your own Kubernetes?
options: The platform manages the cluster infrastructure so you only provide containers
Container PaaS is always slower because it adds extra layers
They only support one programming language per container
